segments.text: On that day you will call your brothers Ammi, and you will call your sisters Ruhamah.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Condemn your mother, condemn her because she is not my wife and I am not her husband. Tell her to stop looking like a prostitute—get rid of the makeup and the provocative clothing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Otherwise I will strip her naked as the day she was born, and make her like a desert, a barren land, and let her die of thirst.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will have no pity on her children, because they are children of prostitution.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For their mother was a prostitute who conceived them in a shameful way. She said, ‘I'll run after my lovers who give me my food and water, my wool and flax and olive oil, and my drink.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: That is why I am going to block her path with thorn bushes, and build a stone wall to stop her so she won't find her way.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When she runs after her lovers she won't catch up with them; she'll look for them but she won't find them. Then she'll say, ‘Let me go back to my former husband, because it was better for me then than now.’
segments.speaker: Israel (metaphorical wife)

segments.text: She doesn't consider that it was me who gave her grain, new wine, and olive oil, nor the silver and the gold which I gave her abundantly that they made into an idol of Baal.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: So I will take back my ripened grain and my new wine I gave at harvest-time. I will take back my wool and my flax I provided to cover her nakedness.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will strip her naked while her lovers watch; no one will be able to rescue her from me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will put a stop to all her festivities: her feasts, new moon celebrations, special Sabbaths—all her festivals.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will destroy her vines and fig trees that she said were given to her as payment for being a prostitute. I will turn them into scrub-land; only wild animals will eat the remaining fruit.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will punish her for all the times she offered incense to Baal, dressing herself up, putting on rings and jewelry, going after her lovers, but forgetting about me, declares the Lord.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: See what I'm going to do! I will win her back, taking her into the desert where I will speak to her heart-to-heart.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There I will return her vineyards to her and make the Valley of Achor a door of hope. There she will respond to me as she did when she was young, as in the time when she left the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then on that day, says the Lord, you will call me ‘my husband’ and not ‘my Baal.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will stop her appealing to the Baals—their names won't be mentioned ever again.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: At that time I will make a solemn agreement with the wild animals and the birds of the sky and the creeping things of the ground. I will do away with the bow and the sword; I will abolish war from the land so you can lie down in safety.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will make you my wife forever; I will make you my wife in goodness and justice and love and mercy.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will be faithful to you, my wife, and you will know me as the Lord.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: On that day, declares the Lord, I will answer the heavens and they will answer the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The earth will answer the grain, and the new wine, and the olive oil, and they shall answer, ‘Jezreel’ (God sows).
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will ‘sow’ her for myself in the earth. I will love Lo-ruhamah (unloved) and I will say ‘You are my people’ to Lo-ammi (not my people), and they will say, ‘You are my God.’
segments.speaker: God
